result.stdout:
Usage: dbup-cli.dll [command] [options]

Options:

  -v | --version
  Show version information

Commands:

  drop              Drops database if exists. SqlServer and AzureSql providers only.
  init              Create a new config file
  mark-as-executed  Mark all scripts as executed
  status            Show upgrade status
  upgrade           Upgrade database

Use "dbup-cli.dll [command] --help" for more information about a command.

commands.payload:

Options:

Commands:

  mark-as-executed  Mark all scripts as executed
  status            Show upgrade status
  upgrade           Upgrade database

Use "dbup-cli.dll [command] --help" for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Drops database if exists. SqlServer and AzureSql providers only.

Usage: dbup-cli.dll drop [options] [<file>]

Arguments:

  file  <TEXT>  [dbup.yml]
  Path to a configuration file. The path can be absolute or relative against a current directory

Options:

  -e | --env (Multiple)  <TEXT>  []
  Path to an environment file. Can be more than one file specified. The path can be absolute or relative against a current directory

result.stdout:
Mark all scripts as executed

Usage: dbup-cli.dll mark-as-executed [options] [<file>]

Arguments:

  file  <TEXT>  [dbup.yml]
  Path to a configuration file. The path can be absolute or relative against a current directory

Options:

  -v | --verbosity       <VERBOSITYLEVEL>  [normal]
  Verbosity level. Can be one of: detail, normal or min
  Allowed values: detail, normal, min

  -e | --env (Multiple)  <TEXT>            []
  Path to an environment file. Can be more than one file specified. The path can be absolute or relative against a current directory

  --ensure
  Create a database if not exists

result.stdout:
Show upgrade status

Usage: dbup-cli.dll status [options] [<file>]

Arguments:

  file  <TEXT>  [dbup.yml]
  Path to a configuration file. The path can be absolute or relative against a current directory

Options:

  -v | --verbosity          <VERBOSITYLEVEL>  [normal]
  Verbosity level. Can be one of: detail, normal or min
  Allowed values: detail, normal, min

  -e | --env (Multiple)     <TEXT>            []
  Path to an environment file. Can be more than one file specified. The path can be absolute or relative against a current directory

  -x | --show-executed
  Print names of executed scripts

  -n | --show-not-executed
  Print names of scripts to be execute
